page: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors operating at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The hardware configuration includes 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, PassMark CPU, and PassMark CPU encryption tests all placing in the top 10% of evaluated systems. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth also ranks in the top 10%, reaching 338.87 GB/sec, while small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth registers at 6698.86 GB/sec. Single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, falls within the middle 50% average.

From a resource density perspective, the r6id.metal offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive memory footprints combined with high-speed local NVMe storage. The inclusion of 7600 GB of direct-attached NVMe SSDs eliminates the need for separate network-attached storage for high-throughput database operations. However, the tradeoff lies in its single-threaded performance, which remains average, meaning workloads that cannot exploit massive parallelism or high memory bandwidth may not fully utilize the hardware's premium profile.

This instance is designed for memory-intensive and data-heavy enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, big data analytics, and high-throughput web serving. The presence of hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector extensions also supports secure, high-performance computing tasks and large-scale software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth, both ranking in the top 10%. While single-core performance remains in the average tier, the instance excels in parallel processing, cryptography, and image processing. This hardware profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense deployments by bundling massive memory with high-capacity local storage. The r6id.metal is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform combining massive DDR4 capacity with high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.

1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 338.87 GB/sec large-block read bandwidth
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and encryption benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
